compositions.name: Cohere Command R+ / Command A Agents
compositions.intent: Enterprise-grade Cohere model family (Command R/R+/A) built so multi-step tool use, RAG with inline citations, and JSON-schema-constrained outputs are first-class API behaviours rather than client-side workarounds.
compositions.description: Cohere markets the Command family as 'AI purpose-built for real-world agentic applications anchored in your data'. The Chat API directly supports multi-step (a.k.a. agentic) tool use: the model can decide to chain tool calls and feed each result into the next step. RAG is a documented mode on the same endpoint, with citations emitted alongside generated text. Structured outputs are constrained either by JSON mode or by a caller-supplied JSON Schema. Cohere also packages Command into the North platform for enterprise agent workflows.
primary_use_cases: enterprise RAG with grounded, inline-cited answers
multi-step tool/function calling agents
JSON-schema-constrained structured extraction
regulated-industry chat backends and copilots
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Stateless HTTP request/response Chat API. The caller drives a tool-use loop: send user message + tools schema, receive either a final answer or a list of tool_calls, execute the tools, post the tool_results back as a new turn, repeat until the model returns a normal assistant message. RAG mode uses a documents[] parameter on the same endpoint and emits citations[] alongside text. Same loop shape supports JSON mode and JSON-Schema-constrained outputs via response_format.

key_concepts.name: Tool use API
key_concepts.summary: Chat-API mechanism to connect Command models to external tools (search, APIs, functions, databases).

key_concepts.name: Multi-step tool use
key_concepts.summary: Model can chain tool calls; the workflow loop runs steps 2 and 3 multiple times until done.

key_concepts.name: Parallel tool calls
key_concepts.summary: Model can return more than one tool call per step — same tool repeated or different tools.

key_concepts.name: RAG with citations
key_concepts.summary: Pass documents in the documents[] parameter; model returns text with inline fine-grained citations.

key_concepts.name: JSON mode / JSON Schema mode
key_concepts.summary: response_format='json_object' guarantees valid JSON; passing a schema constrains the model to it.

key_concepts.name: North
key_concepts.summary: Cohere's enterprise agent platform powered by Command models; used in regulated industries.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  USER[User request] --> CHAT[Cohere Chat API]
  CHAT --> MODE{Mode}
  MODE -->|tool use| TOOL[Model emits tool_calls]
  TOOL --> PARALLEL[Parallel / sequential tool calls]
  PARALLEL --> EXEC[Caller executes tools]
  EXEC -->|tool_results turn| CHAT
  MODE -->|RAG| DOCS[documents parameter]
  DOCS --> GROUND[Grounded answer + inline citations]
  MODE -->|structured| JSON[response_format json_object or JSON Schema]
  JSON --> VALID[Schema-constrained output]
  CHAT --> ANS[Assistant reply]

members.note: RAG is a documented mode on the Chat API; the model generates answers grounded on documents[] and emits inline citations out-of-the-box.

evidence.title: Cohere RAG — grounded generation
evidence.quote: generate text that is grounded on supplementary documents, thus minimizing hallucinations

evidence.title: Cohere RAG — documents parameter
evidence.quote: The Chat endpoint supports a few different options for structuring documents in the `documents` parameter

members.note: Cohere advertises fine-grained citations as a built-in output of the RAG pipeline.

evidence.title: Cohere RAG — inline citations
evidence.quote: Cohere models are trained to generate fine-grained citations, out-of-the-box, alongside their text output

evidence.title: Cohere RAG — example
evidence.quote: The response also includes **inline citations** that reference the first two documents

members.note: Multi-step tool use loop — model decides on a sequence of tool calls, executes each, feeds the observation back. This is the ReAct shape even though Cohere's docs don't use the academic name.

evidence.title: Cohere multi-step tool use
evidence.quote: the model might decide to do a sequence of tool calls in order to answer the user request. This means that steps 2 and 3 will run multiple times in loop.

members.note: JSON mode guarantees a valid JSON object; JSON Schema mode constrains output to a caller-supplied schema.

evidence.title: Cohere structured outputs — JSON mode
evidence.quote: By setting the `response_format` type to `"json_object"` in the Chat API, the output of the model is guaranteed to be a valid JSON object.

evidence.title: Cohere structured outputs — JSON Schema mode
evidence.quote: This forces the LLM to stick to this schema, thus giving you greater control over the output.

members.note: Tool use is a top-level Chat API feature, advertised as the way to connect Command models to external systems.

evidence.title: Cohere tool use overview
evidence.quote: Tool use is a technique which allows developers to connect Cohere's Command family of models to external tools like search engines, APIs, functions, databases, etc.

evidence.title: Cohere Command product page — Tool Use API
evidence.quote: Connect Command to our Tool Use API to build powerful agents and research tools across in-house and cloud applications

members.note: Model can return more than one tool_call per assistant turn — same tool repeated or different tools.

evidence.title: Cohere tool use — multiple calls per step
evidence.quote: The model can determine that more than one tool call is required. This can be calling the same tool multiple times or different tools for any number of calls.

references.title: Command A product page
references.quote: Our most efficient and performant model to date, specializing in agentic AI, multilingual, and human evaluations for real-life use cases

compositions.name: DeepSeek Agent
compositions.intent: OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible Chinese model API (deepseek-chat, deepseek-reasoner) that exposes function calling with a strict JSON-Schema mode, separate reasoning_content channel, and is positioned primarily as a drop-in backend for third-party agent harnesses (Claude Code, Copilot CLI, OpenCode).
compositions.description: DeepSeek does not ship a hosted agent product; instead the API mirrors OpenAI's Chat Completions shape (and is also Anthropic-compatible) so any existing agent harness can swap DeepSeek in as the model. Function calling is supported with up to 128 tools, a strict mode that pins outputs to a JSON Schema, and tool_choice (none/auto/required). The reasoning model deepseek-reasoner emits a separate reasoning_content stream alongside the answer. DeepSeek's own listed agent integrations include Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, GitHub Copilot CLI and others — the company is the model, the agents are the third parties.
primary_use_cases: drop-in backend model for Claude Code / Copilot CLI / OpenCode style agents
schema-strict function calling for tool-using agents
reasoning agents that need a separate chain-of-thought channel
JSON-output extraction pipelines
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Stateless OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible Chat Completions endpoint. The caller drives a standard tools-loop: post messages + tools[], receive either content or tool_calls, execute tools, append tool messages, repeat. For deepseek-reasoner the model also emits a reasoning_content field at the same level as content, which clients are expected to surface or discard. DeepSeek does not host the agent runtime — the harness lives in the caller (Claude Code, etc.).

key_concepts.name: OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible API
key_concepts.summary: DeepSeek's API mirrors OpenAI's and Anthropic's shapes so SDKs and agent harnesses can switch backends by changing config only.

key_concepts.name: Function Calling
key_concepts.summary: Tool-call API with up to 128 functions and tool_choice control (none/auto/required).

key_concepts.name: Strict mode (JSON Schema)
key_concepts.summary: Forces tool-call arguments to match the function's JSON Schema; supports object, string, number, integer, boolean, array, enum, anyOf and $ref/$def.

key_concepts.name: JSON output mode
key_concepts.summary: response_format={'type':'json_object'} returns valid JSON; requires the word 'json' in the prompt plus an example schema.

key_concepts.name: deepseek-reasoner reasoning_content
key_concepts.summary: Reasoning model surfaces a CoT in reasoning_content at the same level as content, separately consumable by clients.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD USER[User request] --> HARNESS[Third-party agent harness
Claude Code / Copilot CLI / OpenCode] HARNESS -->|OpenAI- or Anthropic-shaped/| API[DeepSeek Chat API] API --> MODEL{Model} MODEL -->|deepseek-chat| ANSWER[content] MODEL -->|deepseek-reasoner| COT[reasoning_content + content] MODEL -->|tools enabled| CALL[tool_calls
strict-JSON-schema args] CALL --> HARNESS HARNESS -->|tool results turn| API

members.note: Two complementary mechanisms: response_format json_object for free-form JSON, and strict mode for schema-pinned function-call arguments.

evidence.title: DeepSeek JSON mode
evidence.quote: Set the `response_format` parameter to `{'type': 'json_object'}`.

evidence.title: DeepSeek function calling — strict mode
evidence.quote: In `strict` mode, the model strictly adheres to the format requirements of the Function's JSON schema when outputting a Function call

members.note: Function calling supports up to 128 tools with tool_choice none/auto/required; the user is responsible for executing the function — DeepSeek only emits the call.

evidence.title: DeepSeek function calling — overview
evidence.quote: Function Calling allows the model to call external tools to enhance its capabilities.

evidence.title: DeepSeek function calling — model does not execute
evidence.quote: The functionality of the `get_weather` function needs to be provided by the user. The model itself does not execute specific functions.

evidence.title: DeepSeek chat completion — tool_choice
evidence.quote: `auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools.

members.note: deepseek-reasoner exposes the chain-of-thought in a separate reasoning_content field at the same level as content; clients can display or distill it.

evidence.title: DeepSeek reasoning model — CoT
evidence.quote: Before delivering the final answer, the model first generates a Chain of Thought (CoT) to enhance the accuracy of its responses.

evidence.title: DeepSeek reasoning model — reasoning_content field
evidence.quote: `reasoning_content`: The content of the CoT, which is at the same level as `content` in the output structure.

members.note: DeepSeek positions itself as a drop-in backend behind agent harnesses (Claude Code, Copilot CLI, OpenCode) by exposing OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible API shapes; routing is a config change in the harness.

evidence.title: DeepSeek API — OpenAI/Anthropic compatibility
evidence.quote: The DeepSeek API uses an API format compatible with OpenAI/Anthropic. By modifying the configuration, you can use the OpenAI/Anthropic SDK or softwares compatible with the OpenAI/Anthropic API to access the DeepSeek API.

evidence.title: DeepSeek API — agent integrations
evidence.quote: The DeepSeek API is supported by many popular AI agent and coding assistant tools. If you use tools like Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, or OpenCode, you can use DeepSeek as the backend model directly — no code required.

compositions.name: Doubao Agents
compositions.intent: ByteDance's consumer Doubao chatbot and the Volcano Engine Ark API behind it, offering text/image/video/audio model invocation with documented tool-call building blocks: Function Calling, Web Search, Knowledge Base Search, image processing, and structured output (beta).
compositions.description: Doubao is the consumer ByteDance assistant launched August 2023; by November 2024 it was China's most popular AI chatbot with around 60 million MAU and is powered by ByteDance's Volcano Engine cloud, which the company says processes around 120 trillion tokens per day. Developers reach the same Doubao models through Volcano Engine's Ark platform, whose docs catalogue tool-call building blocks (Function Calling, Web Search a.k.a. 联网内容插件, Knowledge Base Search, image processing) alongside deep thinking, streaming and structured output (beta) modes. The international consumer variant is sold as Cici/Dola and is reportedly powered by Microsoft Azure-hosted OpenAI GPT models rather than Doubao itself.
primary_use_cases: consumer chat assistant (Chinese market, mobile + web)
tool-augmented Doubao model invocation via Ark API
web-search-grounded answers via 联网内容插件
private knowledge base search and document Q&A
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Two surfaces. (1) Consumer Doubao chat app: ByteDance-hosted loop, opaque to users — conversation, search, tool invocations all happen server-side. (2) Volcano Engine Ark API: developer-driven loop similar to other model-vendor APIs — send chat completion request with optional tool definitions and built-in plugins (web search, knowledge base), receive content or tool_calls, execute, return, repeat. Ark also offers deep thinking, streaming, structured-output (beta), context caching and file inputs as request-level toggles.

key_concepts.name: Doubao chat app
key_concepts.summary: ByteDance's consumer AI assistant; web, iOS, Android. ~60M MAU in China as of late 2024.

key_concepts.name: Volcano Engine Ark (火山方舟)
key_concepts.summary: Developer platform exposing Doubao models with managed inference, fine-tuning and deployment.

key_concepts.name: Function Calling (函数调用)
key_concepts.summary: Tool-call API listed under 工具调用 in the Ark docs alongside web search and knowledge base search.

key_concepts.name: Web Search (联网内容插件)
key_concepts.summary: Built-in web-search plugin Ark exposes as a tool option for Doubao models.

key_concepts.name: Knowledge Base Search (私域知识库搜索)
key_concepts.summary: Built-in private knowledge-base retrieval tool the Ark platform offers alongside Doubao chat.

key_concepts.name: Deep thinking + structured output (beta)
key_concepts.summary: Ark request-level toggles for chain-of-thought style reasoning and JSON-shaped outputs.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD USER[User] --> SURFACE{Surface} SURFACE -->|consumer| APP[Doubao chat app
web / iOS / Android] SURFACE -->|developer| ARK[Volcano Engine Ark API] ARK --> MODEL[Doubao model] MODEL --> TOOLS{Tool options} TOOLS --> FC[Function Calling] TOOLS --> WS[Web Search 联网内容插件] TOOLS --> KB[Knowledge Base Search] TOOLS --> IMG[Image processing] MODEL --> MODES{Request toggles} MODES --> DT[Deep thinking] MODES --> ST[Streaming] MODES --> SO[Structured output beta] MODES --> CC[Context caching]

members.note: Ark surfaces Web Search (联网内容插件) and Knowledge Base Search (私域知识库搜索) as tool options for Doubao models, so retrieval can be selected by the model in the same loop as function calls. Detailed implementation documentation is thin on the publicly visible English-translated pages.

evidence.title: Volcano Engine Ark docs — tool palette (Chinese, navigation index)
evidence.quote: [translated] tool options include Doubao assistant, Web Search, image processing, knowledge base search, cloud deployment/MCP

members.note: Function Calling (函数调用) is one of the documented categories under 工具调用 (Tool Calling) in the Volcano Engine Ark docs alongside Web Search and Knowledge Base Search.

evidence.title: Volcano Engine Ark — Function Calling reference
evidence.quote: Function Calling（函数调用）

members.note: Ark docs list 结构化输出 (structured output) as a beta feature alongside streaming, deep thinking and context caching. No public English-language schema reference verified.

evidence.title: Volcano Engine Ark — features list (Chinese)
evidence.quote: [translated] supports deep thinking, context management, streaming output, structured output (beta), context caching and file input

references.title: Wikipedia — Doubao
references.quote: By November 2024, it had become China's most popular AI chatbot, with approximately 60 million monthly active users

compositions.name: Genspark
compositions.intent: Consumer general-purpose 'super agent' / AI workspace blending multi-LLM routing, browser actions, code execution, generated 'Sparkpages' and downstream apps; built and operated by MainFunc.
compositions.description: Genspark markets itself as an AI-powered workspace whose Super Agent autonomously completes user tasks — from research to running multi-step workflows. The company's GitHub publicly showcases Clawverse, claimed to be built by 100+ autonomous coding sprints of 17,500 lines with zero human-written code. Public, citable technical documentation for Genspark's internal agent loop is scarce: most claims about a mixture-of-agents (MoA) or specific tool counts circulate through press/marketing rather than verifiable docs. The product page and several deep technical pages return 403 to unauthenticated scrapers, so several claims are recorded here at evidence_status='none'.
primary_use_cases: consumer research and task agent
generated 'Sparkpages' answer pages
AI-built apps / artifacts (e.g. Clawverse)
broad task automation across browser, search, code
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Hosted consumer agent loop; not documented in citable upstream form. From public surfaces and the GitHub showcase: a user task is decomposed into sub-actions executed against multiple LLMs, a browser surface and code-execution capability; results are aggregated into long-form 'Sparkpages' or app artifacts. Internal details (which models, what routing, what step budget) are not published.

key_concepts.name: Super Agent
key_concepts.summary: Marketed top-level autonomous agent that completes user-defined tasks; one Super Bowl ad described it as a tool that 'completes tasks for that day'.

key_concepts.name: Sparkpages
key_concepts.summary: Generated long-form answer/report pages produced as agent output. Detailed product docs are not publicly fetchable.

key_concepts.name: Clawverse
key_concepts.summary: Showcase project on Genspark's GitHub claimed to be built entirely by autonomous coding sprints.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  USER[User task] --> SUPER[Super Agent]
  SUPER --> ROUTE{Route work}
  ROUTE --> LLMS[Multiple LLMs]
  ROUTE --> BROWSER[Browser surface]
  ROUTE --> CODE[Code execution]
  LLMS --> AGG[Aggregate]
  BROWSER --> AGG
  CODE --> AGG
  AGG --> OUT{Output}
  OUT --> SPARKPAGE[Sparkpage]
  OUT --> APP[App artifact e.g. Clawverse]

members.note: Sparkpages and 'workspace' positioning imply tool-driven retrieval over the open web, but no upstream technical doc was found that describes the retrieval pipeline in detail.
Genspark publishes an Omaha-for-Genspark-browser repo on GitHub, indicating an in-house Chromium-based browser surface, but the technical model of how the agent drives it is not publicly documented.

evidence.title: Genspark AI GitHub — Omaha for Genspark browser
evidence.quote: Omaha for Genspark browser

members.note: The Clawverse showcase claim implies the agent runs and iterates on generated code; details of the execution environment are not in the public docs we could fetch.

evidence.title: Genspark AI GitHub — Clawverse
evidence.quote: Clawverse — Build your pixel island, visit friends, collect everything. Built entirely by Genspark AI — 100+ autonomous coding sprints, 17,500 lines of code, zero human-written code.

members.note: Press reporting commonly describes Genspark as using a Mixture-of-Agents / multi-LLM routing approach; no verifiable upstream Genspark engineering doc was reachable to confirm this directly.
Claimed as part of Super Agent's surface area in marketing; no verifiable upstream technical doc reached.
Implied by the Super Agent + sub-tools framing; not documented in citable upstream sources.
Implied by autonomous task completion; not documented in citable upstream sources.
Implied by the 'super agent over sub-agents' framing; not documented in citable upstream sources.
Tool use is central to the Super Agent claim; specific tool catalogue and invocation contract not publicly documented.

compositions.name: Kimi (Moonshot)
compositions.intent: Moonshot's Kimi assistant and the open-weight Kimi K2 model family — a long-context (256K) MoE model explicitly trained for tool calling and agentic behaviour, surfaced through the Kimi consumer product line (Deep Research, Agent Swarm, Kimi Code, OK Computer).
compositions.description: Kimi began in 2023 as Moonshot AI's chatbot known for 128K-token context; subsequent versions raised the window to 256K and added multimodal text/image/video input. Kimi K2 (released July 2025) is the open-weight 1T-total / 32B-active MoE model behind the product, explicitly trained for tool use and autonomous problem-solving and licensed permissively. The Kimi consumer surface bundles long-context chat with Deep Research, Agent Swarm, Slides/Docs/Sheets/Websites generation, and a sub-product called OK Computer that can build multi-page sites and editable slides from prompts and process up to 1M rows of input data. Public English documentation of the underlying API's tool-call schema redirects between several domains (platform.moonshot.cn → platform.kimi.com → platform.kimi.ai) and several pages were not directly fetchable; primary technical evidence here comes from the Kimi K2 model documentation.
primary_use_cases: long-context document analysis and Q&A
agentic tool-calling deployments via Kimi K2 open weights
Deep Research and Agent Swarm consumer workflows
AI-built slides, websites, sheets and docs from prompts
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Two surfaces. (1) Kimi consumer app: Moonshot-hosted assistant; user starts a chat or invokes a sub-product (Deep Research, Agent Swarm, OK Computer); model autonomously calls internal tools and produces multi-modal artifacts. (2) Kimi K2 API: standard chat-completions tool-call loop where the model autonomously decides when and how to invoke tools, designed for drop-in general chat and agentic experiences.

key_concepts.name: Kimi K2
key_concepts.summary: Open-weight 1T total / 32B active MoE model (384 experts, 8 routed per token) trained on 15.5T tokens; designed for tool use and autonomous problem-solving.

key_concepts.name: Long-context window (256K)
key_concepts.summary: Kimi K2 / Kimi 2.6 supports 256K tokens of context, with K2 documentation also listing 128K base.

key_concepts.name: Agent Swarm
key_concepts.summary: Product feature in the Kimi consumer app advertised on the home page alongside Deep Research, Kimi Code and others.

key_concepts.name: Deep Research
key_concepts.summary: Kimi consumer research mode; the older Kimi Explore Edition reportedly deeply reads 500+ pages per search.

key_concepts.name: OK Computer
key_concepts.summary: Kimi sub-product that builds multi-page websites and editable slides from prompts and processes up to 1M rows of input data.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD USER[User] --> SURFACE{Surface} SURFACE -->|consumer| KIMI[Kimi chat app] KIMI --> SUBS[Sub-products] SUBS --> DR[Deep Research] SUBS --> SWARM[Agent Swarm] SUBS --> CODE[Kimi Code] SUBS --> OK[OK Computer
sites + slides] SURFACE -->|API| K2[Kimi K2 open-weight MoE
1T total / 32B active] K2 --> CTX[256K context] K2 --> TOOLS[Autonomous tool-calling] TOOLS --> APP[Caller-side agent harness]

members.note: Kimi Explore / Deep Research is positioned as autonomous AI-powered search reading hundreds of pages per query; specific RAG plumbing details are not in the verified upstream English docs we could reach.

evidence.quote: OK Computer...is capable of creating multi-page websites and editable slides from simple user prompts and can process up to 1 million rows of input data at once.

members.note: Long context is Kimi's signature: initial 128K, later 256K.

evidence.quote: increased its context window from 128K tokens to 256K tokens.

evidence.quote: Kimi-K2-Instruct has strong tool-calling capabilities.

evidence.title: Kimi K2 repo — autonomous tool selection
evidence.quote: the model will autonomously decide when and how to invoke them.

compositions.name: Manus
compositions.intent: Provide a general-purpose autonomous AI agent that completes end-to-end knowledge work in a dedicated cloud sandbox — browsing, executing code, editing files, tracking a todo.md plan — and fanning out into hundreds of parallel sub-agent clones when a task suits wide-rather-than-deep work.
compositions.description: Manus, originally launched in March 2025 and acquired by Meta in December 2025, is a hosted general AI agent: every task is given its own isolated cloud VM in which the agent reads/writes a todo.md plan, drives a browser, runs Python/JavaScript/Bash, and operates a Linux filesystem. The product pioneered the Wide Research / clone fan-out pattern, in which a main Manus instance spawns up to ~100 fully-capable sub-agents — each a general-purpose Manus, each in its own context window — to process bulk similar items in parallel. The team has publicly documented their context engineering practice — KV-cache stability, file-system-as-context, masking tool logits instead of hot-swapping tool definitions — as the operational core of the loop. The company continues to operate from Singapore after joining Meta.
primary_use_cases: general-purpose autonomous knowledge work in a hosted sandbox
Wide Research / clone fan-out tasks over hundreds of similar items
long-horizon agent runs needing browser + code + file tools
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Imperative ReAct-style outer loop driving a long-lived cloud VM. Each task allocated its own Manus Sandbox (fully-isolated Ubuntu VM with networking, filesystem, headless Chromium, shell). One action per iteration, executed in-sandbox, observation appended, cycle repeats. Manus typically writes and maintains a todo.md plan inside the sandbox so the current goal stays at the recent end of the context. Typical task ~50 iterations. Sandboxes persist for hours, auto-sleep when idle, auto-wake on user return. Wide Research sub-protocol spawns N parallel sub-agents, each itself a general-purpose Manus in its own context.

key_concepts.name: Manus Sandbox
key_concepts.summary: Fully-isolated cloud VM allocated per task — networking, filesystem, browser, software tools.

key_concepts.name: todo.md plan file
key_concepts.summary: Persistent markdown plan the agent authors and updates step-by-step inside the sandbox; keeps the current goal near the end of context.

key_concepts.name: Wide Research / 副本扇出
key_concepts.summary: Protocol to spawn dozens-to-hundreds of full-capability Manus clones in parallel; each clone gets its own context and depth equal to the main agent's.

key_concepts.name: Manus Browser Operator
key_concepts.summary: Browser extension letting Manus operate directly inside the user's local browser environment.

key_concepts.name: File system as ultimate context
key_concepts.summary: Manus treats the sandbox filesystem as unlimited, persistent, directly-operable context that the agent reads and writes instead of stuffing the prompt.

key_concepts.name: Tool-masking state machine
key_concepts.summary: Rather than hot-swapping tool definitions mid-task (which breaks KV cache), Manus masks tool-name token logits during decoding to constrain available actions.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD USER[User task] --> MAIN[Manus main instance] MAIN --> SB[Manus Sandbox
per-task cloud VM] SB --> TODO[todo.md plan
append + check-off] SB --> BROWSER[Chromium browser] SB --> SHELL[Bash / Python / JS] SB --> FS[(Filesystem
persistent context)] MAIN -->|loop ~50 iters| ACT{Action selection
tool-masking state machine} ACT --> BROWSER ACT --> SHELL ACT --> FS ACT -->|observation + failures kept| MAIN SB -.->|auto-sleep/wake| SB MAIN -.->|Wide Research fan-out| CLONES[N parallel Manus clones
each full-capability, own context] CLONES -.->|synthesise results| MAIN

members.note: Sandboxes auto-sleep on inactivity and auto-wake on user return; files/data are preserved across the sleep/wake cycle. E2B sandboxes can run for hours in persistent sessions.

evidence.title: Manus Sandbox — sleep/wake
evidence.quote: When the Sandbox is inactive (no operations, file edits, etc.), it will automatically go to sleep. When you return to the task and Manus needs to operate the Sandbox, it will be automatically woken up. During the sleep/wake cycle, the files and data in the Sandbox remain unchanged.

evidence.title: E2B — Manus persistent sessions
evidence.quote: E2B sandboxes can run for hours in persistent sessions while the agent decides in each iteration which action to perform.

members.note: Browser Operator extension drives the user's local browser; Manus 1.5's integrated browser also runs end-to-end test/fix loops against what it builds.

evidence.title: Manus Browser Operator
evidence.quote: a browser extension that enables Manus to operate directly within your local browser environment.

evidence.title: Manus 1.5 release — integrated browser
evidence.quote: With its integrated browser, Manus can actively test what it builds — launching the application, interacting with it like a real user, detecting issues, and fixing them autonomously

members.note: Manus Sandbox is a full cloud VM (networking, filesystem, browser, shell) supporting Python/JavaScript/Bash execution alongside browser control.

evidence.title: Manus Sandbox — fully isolated VM
evidence.quote: Manus Sandbox is a fully isolated cloud virtual machine that Manus allocates for each task.

evidence.title: Manus Sandbox — capabilities
evidence.quote: It has full capabilities: networking, file system, browser, various software tools.

evidence.title: E2B — Manus executors
evidence.quote: agents can run Python, JavaScript, Bash, and more

members.note: Per-iteration single action chosen from a predefined action space, executed in the sandbox, observation appended; standard ReAct loop.

evidence.title: Context Engineering — ReAct loop
evidence.quote: After receiving a user input, the agent proceeds through a chain of tool uses to complete the task. In each iteration, the model selects an action from a predefined action space based on the current context. The action is then executed in the environment (e.g., Manus's virtual machine sandbox) to produce an observation.

members.note: Manus does not expose a user-facing hard step cap, but its loop is dimensioned in iterations — the published anchor is ~50 tool calls per typical task. Downgraded from first-class because there is no exposed knob.

evidence.title: Context Engineering — typical task length
evidence.quote: A typical task in Manus requires around 50 tool calls on average.

members.note: Wide Research sub-agents are full-capability general-purpose Manus instances, each with its own dedicated context window. Sandboxes also isolate tasks from each other at the VM level.

evidence.title: Wide Research — full-capability sub-agents
evidence.quote: every subagent in Wide Research is a fully capable, general-purpose Manus instance.

evidence.title: Wide Research 中文 docs — per-project context (Mandarin)
evidence.quote: 项目 #250 获得了与项目 #1 相同的分析深度，因为每个项目都有自己的专用 Agent 和完整的上下文窗口

evidence.title: Manus Sandbox — VM-level isolation
evidence.quote: Each Sandbox runs in its own environment, does not affect other tasks, and can execute in parallel.

members.note: Tool palette includes Chromium browser, terminal, filesystem; tool availability is regulated by a context-aware state machine that masks token logits rather than removing tool definitions.

evidence.title: Context Engineering — tool-masking state machine
evidence.quote: Manus uses a context-aware state machine to manage tool availability. Rather than removing tools, it masks the token logits during decoding to prevent (or enforce) the selection of certain actions based on the current context.

evidence.title: E2B — Manus tool palette
evidence.quote: using the Chromium browser to visit URLs, save images, and scroll, through executing terminal commands to using the filesystem to create, edit, or delete files

members.note: Wide Research is Manus's signature feature: a system-level mechanism for parallel processing and an agent-to-agent collaboration protocol that runs ~20 sub-tasks (or hundreds) simultaneously.

evidence.title: Introducing Wide Research
evidence.quote: Wide Research is a system-level mechanism for parallel processing, and a protocol for agent-to-agent collaboration.

evidence.title: Help Center — Wide Research
evidence.quote: Wide Research can run 20 subtasks simultaneously, equivalent to having 20 Agents helping with one task.

evidence.title: Wide Research 中文 docs — parallel scale (Mandarin)
evidence.quote: 数百个并行工作的独立 Agent

members.note: Manus authors a todo.md and updates it step-by-step; the file keeps the current goal at the recent end of context.

evidence.title: Context Engineering — todo.md plan
evidence.quote: when handling complex tasks, it tends to create a todo.md file—and update it step-by-step as the task progresses, checking off completed items.

anti_patterns_avoided.note: Manus deliberately leaves failed actions and stack traces in the context so the model can adapt; erasing failure removes the evidence the model needs to learn from.

evidence.title: Context Engineering — keep failures in context
evidence.quote: When the model sees a failed action—and the resulting observation or stack trace—it implicitly updates its internal beliefs.

anti_patterns_avoided.note: Wide Research sub-agents are deliberately NOT typed as manager/coder/designer; each is a full-capability general-purpose Manus.

evidence.title: Wide Research — vs role-typed multi-agent
evidence.quote: predefined roles (like 'manager', 'coder', or 'designer')

alternatives.note: Open-source clone reproducing Manus's browser + sandbox + plan-file loop.
Cognition's autonomous AI engineer with a sandbox + browser + plan loop; closest US competitor pre-Manus.
Anthropic's computer-use loop targets the same hosted-VM-plus-browser shape; Manus 1.5 explicitly integrates the Agent Skills standard.

references.title: Context Engineering for AI Agents — Lessons from Building Manus
Manus joins Meta for next era of innovation

compositions.name: MiniMax Agent
compositions.intent: Shanghai-based MiniMax bundles a foundation-model line (ABAB → MiniMax-01 → M1 → M2.x) with a consumer MiniMax Agent that evaluates tasks, assembles an 'Agent Team' for them, and learns user-specific habits into custom skills.
compositions.description: MiniMax was founded in 2021 by SenseTime alumni and listed in Hong Kong in January 2026. It develops a series of multimodal foundation models with strong code and Agent capabilities; the company markets MiniMax-M1 as the world's first open-source, large-scale, hybrid-attention reasoning model with an 80K-token reasoning output and tool-use benchmark leadership among open-weights. MiniMax Agent is the consumer surface: an assistant that evaluates a task, builds an agent team, learns user habits, and turns repetitive workflows into custom skills. The API exposes function calling for tool use; verifiable English platform docs were patchy at access time (several pages 404 or redirect repeatedly between intl.minimaxi.com / platform.minimax.io).
primary_use_cases: consumer multi-skill assistant (work and life tasks)
agent-team task decomposition over arbitrary user goals
open-weight reasoning model for self-hosted tool-use agents
long-output reasoning (80K token thinking budget)
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Two surfaces. (1) MiniMax Agent product: hosted loop that evaluates the user task, assembles an Agent Team, and dispatches sub-tasks; surfaces skills, memory and schedules in a single chat. (2) MiniMax API (M1 / M2.x via Chat Completions): standard tool-call loop; reasoning models expose a long 80K-token thinking budget and benchmark in TAU-bench style agentic tool-use scenarios.

key_concepts.name: MiniMax-M1 reasoning model
key_concepts.summary: First open-source large-scale hybrid-attention reasoning model with 80K reasoning tokens and strong agentic tool-use benchmarks.

key_concepts.name: MiniMax-M2.x line
key_concepts.summary: Newer self-improvement-oriented reasoning + agent model line announced for production agent harnesses.

key_concepts.name: Agent Team
key_concepts.summary: Product feature where MiniMax Agent evaluates a task and assembles a team of agents to solve it.

key_concepts.name: Custom Skills
key_concepts.summary: Repetitive workflows the agent learns from the user's habits and turns into reusable skills.

key_concepts.name: Function Calling
key_concepts.summary: Tool-use API on the MiniMax platform; documented under the Function Calling endpoint reference.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD USER[User task] --> AGENT[MiniMax Agent chat] AGENT --> EVAL[Evaluate task] EVAL --> TEAM[Assemble Agent Team] TEAM --> WORKERS[Sub-agents / skills] WORKERS --> RESULT[Result] AGENT --> SKILLS[(Custom Skills
learnt from habits)] AGENT --> MODEL{Backing model} MODEL -->|reasoning| M1[MiniMax-M1
80K reasoning tokens] MODEL -->|production agent| M2[MiniMax-M2.x] MODEL --> TOOLS[Function calling] TOOLS -->|tool_calls + results| AGENT

members.note: Function calling with JSON-typed arguments is the documented platform mechanism for structured tool output. A dedicated English structured-output reference page was not reliably reachable at access time.

evidence.title: MiniMax platform — function-calling reference URL
evidence.quote: function-calling

members.note: Documented as a top-level API capability ('agent tool use scenarios' in M1 release) and as a benchmarked competence of the open-weight models.

evidence.title: MiniMax-M1 release — agentic tool use
evidence.quote: In agent tool use scenarios (TAU-bench), MiniMax-M1-40k also leads all open-weight models and outperforms Gemini-2.5 Pro

evidence.title: MiniMax company overview — agent capabilities
evidence.quote: independently developed a series of multimodal foundation models with powerful code and Agent capabilities

members.note: MiniMax Agent's headline product behaviour is 'Build Agent Team: Evaluate tasks and assemble teams to solve problems' — an orchestrator-over-workers shape.

evidence.title: MiniMax Agent product page
evidence.quote: Build Agent Team: Evaluate tasks and assemble teams to solve problems.

members.note: 'Know Your Habits' turns repetitive tasks into custom skills accessible from the same chat surface.

evidence.title: MiniMax Agent — custom skills
evidence.quote: Know Your Habits: Learn how you work and turn repetitive tasks into custom skills.

members.note: MiniMax-M1 surfaces an 80K-token reasoning output budget, claimed to be an industry leader for reasoning length.

evidence.title: MiniMax-M1 release — 80K reasoning output
evidence.quote: includes an industry-leading 80,000 token reasoning output

evidence.title: MiniMax-M1 release — hybrid attention reasoning model
evidence.quote: the world's first open-source, large-scale, hybrid-attention reasoning model

references.title: Wikipedia — MiniMax (company)
references.quote: MiniMax Group Inc. is an artificial intelligence (AI) company based in Shanghai, China.

compositions.name: Trae
compositions.intent: ByteDance's AI IDE family: the Trae IDE with a unified Chat-Builder interface and @Agent + MCP multi-agent system, the autonomous TRAE SOLO coding agent, and the open-source Trae Agent CLI that topped SWE-bench Verified.
compositions.description: Trae is ByteDance's AI coding stack. The IDE provides a unified Chat-Builder interface and an @Agent system with MCP for multi-agent collaboration. TRAE SOLO is positioned as a more autonomous responsive coding agent for real-world software development, with a mobile variant added in 2026. Separately, the open-source Trae Agent CLI (github.com/bytedance/trae-agent) ranked #1 on SWE-bench Verified with Claude 4 and provides a transparent, modular architecture researchers can extend; it supports MCP servers and a toolkit of file editing, bash execution and 'sequential thinking'.
primary_use_cases: AI-assisted coding inside the Trae IDE
autonomous coding sessions via TRAE SOLO
research and benchmarking via the open-source Trae Agent CLI
MCP-driven multi-agent code workflows
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Three surfaces. (1) Trae IDE Chat-Builder: user task in chat is routed to @Agent which composes tool calls and (optionally) sub-agents over MCP. (2) TRAE SOLO: more autonomous loop that executes longer, higher-trust workflows responsively. (3) Trae Agent CLI: a Python ReAct-style CLI taking a natural-language task, running tools (file editing, bash execution, sequential thinking) and optional MCP servers, until the task is done.

key_concepts.name: Chat-Builder unified interface
key_concepts.summary: The Trae IDE's single surface combining conversational chat with a builder mode that produces concrete changes.

key_concepts.name: @Agent system
key_concepts.summary: Mention-based agent invocation that lets the user (or another agent) call a specific agent and pass context.

key_concepts.name: MCP multi-agent collaboration
key_concepts.summary: MCP is the protocol Trae uses to compose multiple agents and external tool servers.

key_concepts.name: TRAE SOLO
key_concepts.summary: Autonomous coding agent variant positioned for real-world software work; mobile variant added 2026.

key_concepts.name: Trae Agent CLI
key_concepts.summary: Open-source Python CLI for general software-engineering tasks; tops SWE-bench Verified.

key_concepts.name: Sequential thinking tool
key_concepts.summary: Built-in 'thinking' tool inside the Trae Agent toolkit alongside file editing and bash execution.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD USER[User] --> SURFACE{Surface} SURFACE -->|IDE| IDE[Trae IDE
Chat-Builder] SURFACE -->|autonomous| SOLO[TRAE SOLO] SURFACE -->|open-source| CLI[Trae Agent CLI] IDE --> AGENTSYS[@Agent system] AGENTSYS --> MCP[MCP servers / sub-agents] CLI --> LOOP[ReAct loop] LOOP --> TOOLS[file edit / bash / sequential thinking] LOOP --> MCP TOOLS --> LOOP MCP --> LOOP

members.note: Trae IDE is an Electron-based agent-aware IDE; the CLI variant provides a transparent agent-computer interface for software-engineering tasks.

evidence.title: Trae Agent README — CLI for software engineering
evidence.quote: Trae Agent is an LLM-based agent for general purpose software engineering tasks. It provides a powerful CLI interface

evidence.title: Trae blog — Chat-Builder
evidence.quote: unveils major updates: a unified Chat-Builder interface, @Agent system with MCP for multi-agent collaboration

members.note: MCP is documented both as the IDE's protocol for multi-agent collaboration and as a configuration-level integration point in the open-source CLI.

evidence.title: Trae Agent README — MCP config
evidence.quote: To enable Model Context Protocol (MCP) services, add an `mcp_servers` section to your configuration

evidence.title: Trae blog — @Agent + MCP
evidence.quote: @Agent system with MCP for multi-agent collaboration, and smarter context handling

members.note: Trae Agent CLI executes complex software-engineering workflows via an explicit tool-call loop with reasoning and structured thinking; same shape underlies the IDE's @Agent.

evidence.title: Trae Agent README — workflow execution
evidence.quote: It provides a powerful CLI interface that can understand natural language instructions and execute complex software engineering workflows

evidence.title: Trae Agent README — tool ecosystem
evidence.quote: Rich Tool Ecosystem: File editing, bash execution, sequential thinking, and more

members.note: Trae IDE markets @Agent + MCP as multi-agent collaboration, implying multiple agents in distinct contexts, but specific isolation guarantees per sub-agent are not spelled out in the public docs we could verify.

evidence.title: Trae blog — multi-agent collaboration
evidence.quote: @Agent system with MCP for multi-agent collaboration, and smarter context handling

members.note: Documented tool palette includes file editing, bash execution, and sequential thinking, plus any MCP servers added to the configuration.

evidence.title: Trae Agent README — toolkit
evidence.quote: Trae Agent provides a comprehensive toolkit for software engineering tasks including file editing, bash execution, structured thinking

references.title: Trae Agent #1 on SWE-bench Verified
references.quote: Trae Agent reached #1 on SWE-bench Verified, with Claude 4 — and it's open-sourced.

compositions.name: Zhipu GLM Agent
compositions.license: proprietary (hosted API) / MIT (GLM open weights since July 2025)
compositions.intent: Tsinghua-spinoff Zhipu (rebranded internationally as Z.ai in July 2025) ships the GLM model line through the BigModel platform, with function calling, web search, knowledge-base retrieval and JSON output all available as tool/response options on the same chat-completion API; AutoGLM is the consumer agent built on top.
compositions.description: Zhipu (Z.ai) is one of China's three biggest LLM providers and IPO'd on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on 8 January 2026. Its GLM model line has been MIT-open-source since July 2025 (GLM-4.5 onward), with the latest GLM-5/5.1 and GLM-5V-Turbo positioned for agentic engineering, coding and long-chain execution. The BigModel chat-completion API supports tools: function calling, knowledge-base retrieval and web search are listed together as tool options, and structured output is selectable via response_format json_object. AutoGLM is the consumer agent application that uses voice commands to complete tasks on a phone and can self-execute operations exceeding 50 steps across applications.
primary_use_cases: function-calling agents over GLM hosted API
RAG via knowledge-base retrieval and web search as built-in tools
JSON-structured extraction via response_format
consumer voice agent (AutoGLM) on smartphones
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Two surfaces. (1) BigModel chat-completion API: standard tools-loop where tools[] can include functions, knowledge-base retrieval, or web search; the model emits tool_calls, the caller executes, results return as tool messages; response_format json_object pins shape. (2) AutoGLM: hosted consumer agent that takes voice commands, plans multi-step actions, and self-executes >50-step workflows across smartphone apps.

key_concepts.name: GLM-4.6 / GLM-5 / GLM-5.1
key_concepts.summary: Flagship Zhipu models; GLM-4.6 raises context to 200K, GLM-5/5.1 positioned as base models for agentic engineering with open-source SOTA-level coding.

key_concepts.name: Tools parameter (function/retrieval/web search)
key_concepts.summary: Single tools parameter accepts function calls, knowledge-base retrieval, and web search; model emits a JSON-typed call the caller handles.

key_concepts.name: response_format json_object
key_concepts.summary: Forces the model to return structured JSON; documented as suitable for extraction and structured API responses.

key_concepts.name: AutoGLM
key_concepts.summary: Consumer agent application that uses voice commands to complete tasks; can self-execute operations exceeding 50 steps across smartphone apps.

key_concepts.name: GLM-5-Turbo for agent harnesses
key_concepts.summary: Trained at the training level for core agent capabilities — tool calling, instruction following, long-chain execution — targeted at agent runtimes ('Claw').

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  USER[User] --> SURFACE{Surface}
  SURFACE -->|API| API[BigModel chat completion]
  API --> TOOLS[tools parameter]
  TOOLS --> FC[function call]
  TOOLS --> KB[knowledge base retrieval]
  TOOLS --> WS[web search]
  API --> FMT[response_format json_object]
  API --> GLM[GLM-4.x / 5.x / 5V-Turbo]
  SURFACE -->|consumer| AUTO[AutoGLM]
  AUTO --> VOICE[voice commands]
  AUTO --> STEPS[>50-step cross-app execution]

members.note: Web search and knowledge-base retrieval are listed together with function calling as values the tools[] parameter accepts — retrieval is model-callable, not a separate API.

evidence.title: BigModel chat-completion docs — tools palette (Chinese)
evidence.quote: 模型可以调用的工具列表。支持函数调用、知识库检索和网络搜索。使用此参数提供模型可以生成 `JSON` 输入的函数列表

members.note: response_format json_object is a top-level toggle that constrains the model to valid JSON.

evidence.title: BigModel chat-completion docs — json_object (Chinese)
evidence.quote: { "type": "json_object" } 表示`JSON`输出模式，模型会返回有效的`JSON`格式数据，适用于结构化数据提取、`API`响应生成等场景

members.note: Function calling is a documented chat-completion capability; the model emits a function name + JSON arguments for the caller to execute.

evidence.title: BigModel chat-completion docs — tool calls (Chinese)
evidence.quote: 生成的应该被调用的工具信息，流式返回时会逐步生成

evidence.title: BigModel chat-completion docs — function call output (Chinese)
evidence.quote: 生成的函数名称和 `JSON` 格式参数

members.note: AutoGLM is positioned as a voice-driven smartphone agent that self-executes >50-step cross-application workflows; this is a separate consumer surface from the BigModel API.

evidence.title: Z.ai — AutoGLM
evidence.quote: An agent model capable of autonomous planning, reasoning, and execution, addressing core challenges in task planning, data scarcity, and policy optimization

evidence.title: Z.ai — AutoGLM step count
evidence.quote: AutoGLM can self-execute operations exceeding 50 steps and execute tasks across applications

evidence.title: Wikipedia — Z.ai / AutoGLM
evidence.quote: an AI agent application that uses voice commands to complete tasks within a smartphone
